---
title: "Books"
---

## Undesirable Immigrants {#undesirable-immigrants}

*Undesirable Immigrants: Why Racism Persists in International Migration*.
Princeton University Press, 2022. International History and Politics series.

[Princeton University Press](https://press.princeton.edu/) ·
[Amazon](https://www.amazon.com/Undesirable-Immigrants-Persists-International-Migration/dp/0691238537)

> **Best Book Award, 2023**, Race, Ethnicity, and Politics section, American
> Political Science Association.

A book about why international migration remains stratified by race long after
the formal end of racially explicit immigration laws. Drawing on quantitative
analysis of bilateral migration, visa, and asylum policies — together with
historical case material — *Undesirable Immigrants* argues that racial
hierarchy has been reproduced through ostensibly neutral administrative
practice.

Profiled in [*Mother Jones*](https://www.motherjones.com/) and discussed on
the *New Books in Political Science* podcast.

---

## Hierarchies of Worth {#hierarchies-of-worth}

*Hierarchies of Worth: Race and the Politics of Costly State Action.* In
progress.

Develops a theory of why some crises generate extraordinary state action while
others produce neglect, exclusion, or procedural restraint. Examines the
interaction between economic scarcity, foreign-policy incentives, and
racialized moral worth judgments, extending debates on race and the liberal
international order.
